backlog.title: Multi-OS Map Selection (opt-in overlay)
backlog.description: Add per-OS command/layer maps selected by the detected host OS, with the default map as a per-track fallback. This is a strict opt-in overlay layered on the green plan-001 codebase: a default-only keymap (zero DEFINE_*_OS macros) MUST be byte-for-byte equivalent to the pre-multi-OS firmware. The OS signal is pushed into the module by the keymap via notifier_set_os(os_variant_t); the module never calls detected_host_os() itself (no link dependency on the OS-detection subsystem). Covers PRD §2 F8/F9, §5.5, §8.6-§8.7, §10.3, §13 invariants 15-21. The pattern matcher, rules.mk, wire protocol, and 9-suite corpus are UNTOUCHED.

milestones.title: Multi-OS API & Dispatch Core
milestones.description: The header surface (notifier.h OS macros + decls), the header-only stub that lets notifier.c compile in the host test harness (qmk_stubs/os_detection.h), and the notifier.c state machine / dispatch changes (current_os global, per-OS weak accessors + selectors, notifier_set_os, OS-first/default-fallback scan in process_full_message). After this milestone, the module compiles and the multi-OS dispatch logic exists, but has no dedicated test coverage yet.

tasks.title: notifier.h OS API surface
tasks.description: Extend the existing notifier.h (~48 lines) with the OS-detection include, the notifier_set_os declaration, and the two DEFINE_SERIAL_*_OS map-definition macros that use ##os token-paste. The header grows to ~80 lines per PRD §5.1/§5.2/§5.5.

subtasks.title: Add os_detection.h include, notifier_set_os decl, and DEFINE_SERIAL_*_OS macros to notifier.h
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: The current notifier.h (read at repo root, 48 lines) has `#include <stdbool.h>` on line 2, followed by the typedefs. The macros DEFINE_SERIAL_COMMANDS/DEFINE_SERIAL_LAYERS already exist and define `user_command_map[]` + `get_command_map()`/`get_command_map_size()` accessors. The new _OS macros follow the SAME pattern but emit per-OS symbols via ##os token-paste. See plan/002_c243e735980a/architecture/external_deps.md for the exact os_variant_t enum values.
2. INPUT: The os_variant_t enum from os_detection.h (provided by dependency P1.M1.T2.S1 — the qmk_stubs stub for host tests, or the real QMK header in production). The enumerator names are OS_UNSURE, OS_LINUX, OS_WINDOWS, OS_MACOS, OS_IOS.
3. LOGIC: Implement PRD §5.1/§5.2/§5.5 exactly:
   (a) Add `#include "os_detection.h"` immediately AFTER `#include <stdbool.h>` (PRD §5.1) so os_variant_t is visible to the notifier_set_os decl and to any keymap that includes notifier.h.
   (b) Add `void notifier_set_os(os_variant_t os);` to the map-accessor declaration block (after `size_t get_layer_map_size(void);`), per PRD §5.2. Add a comment noting it is the ONLY public OS entry point and is pushed from the keymap (§2 F8.2).
   (c) Add the two OS-specific map-definition macros from PRD §5.5 VERBATIM:
       DEFINE_SERIAL_COMMANDS_OS(os, ...) — emits `_notifier_command_map_##os[]`, `_notifier_command_map_##os##_size`, `_notifier_get_command_map_##os()`, `_notifier_get_command_map_##os##_size()`.
       DEFINE_SERIAL_LAYERS_OS(os, ...) — same pattern for layer_map_t: `_notifier_layer_map_##os[]`, etc.
   (d) These macros go AFTER the existing DEFINE_SERIAL_COMMANDS/DEFINE_SERIAL_LAYERS macros (do not reorder existing macros).
   IMPORTANT NAMING CONTRACT: the ##os paste takes the enumerator TOKEN (e.g. caller writes `OS_MACOS`), so generated symbols are `_notifier_command_map_OS_MACOS`, `_notifier_get_command_map_OS_MACOS`, etc. These EXACT names must match the weak defaults in notifier.c (P1.M1.T3.S1) and the select_*_map_os() switch (P1.M1.T3.S1). A mismatch = link failure.
   Row-struct parity: a DEFINE_SERIAL_COMMANDS_OS row has the same shape as DEFINE_SERIAL_COMMANDS (`{ pattern, on_enable, on_disable, case_sensitive? }`); omitted trailing fields zero-fill (case_sensitive -> false). Same for layers.
4. OUTPUT: notifier.h exposes os_detection.h, notifier_set_os, and both DEFINE_SERIAL_*_OS macros. A keymap that writes `DEFINE_SERIAL_COMMANDS_OS(OS_MACOS, { ... })` at file scope generates the 4 per-OS symbols that override notifier.c's weak defaults at link time. Per-OS accessor symbols are an INTERNAL linkage contract and are intentionally NOT declared in this header (PRD §5.5 note).
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Add inline comments on the _OS macros explaining: (a) the ##os token-paste naming contract (generated symbols must match notifier.c select_*_map_os and weak defaults), (b) that OS_UNSURE has no OS-specific map by design (§2 F8.6), (c) row-struct parity with the default macros, (d) the selection rule restated at the API surface (OS map scanned first, default is fallback, per PRD §5.5 selection-rule note). These comments ride WITH the macro definitions — do not create a separate docs subtask.
6. MOCKING: None — this is a pure header (declarations + macros). It compiles in both the QMK build (real os_detection.h) and the host stub harness (qmk_stubs/os_detection.h from P1.M1.T2.S1).

tasks.title: qmk_stubs/os_detection.h (header-only enum stub)
tasks.description: Create a NEW minimal header-only stub of QMK's os_detection.h containing ONLY the os_variant_t enum, so that notifier.c (which now #includes os_detection.h via notifier.h) compiles in the host stub-compile harness. The module uses the TYPE only — it never calls detected_host_os() — so no implementation is needed.

subtasks.title: Write qmk_stubs/os_detection.h with the os_variant_t enum
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Verified from qmk/qmk_firmware `quantum/os_detection.h` (read via GitHub). The REAL header defines the enum as `typedef enum { OS_UNSURE, OS_LINUX, OS_WINDOWS, OS_MACOS, OS_IOS } os_variant_t;` and also declares detected_host_os(void), process_detected_host_os_kb(os_variant_t), etc. The real header begins with `#include "usb_device_state.h"`. See plan/002_c243e735980a/architecture/external_deps.md (verified verbatim).
2. INPUT: None (this is the leaf dependency — it provides the os_variant_t type that P1.M1.T1.S1 and P1.M1.T3.S1 consume).
3. LOGIC: Create file `qmk_stubs/os_detection.h` with:
   - `#pragma once`
   - `#include <stdint.h>` (for any uint types the stubs might need; harmless)
   - The os_variant_t enum VERBATIM matching QMK:
     ```c
     typedef enum {
         OS_UNSURE,   /* = 0 */
         OS_LINUX,    /* = 1 */
         OS_WINDOWS,  /* = 2 */
         OS_MACOS,    /* = 3 */
         OS_IOS,      /* = 4 */
     } os_variant_t;
     ```
   - Do NOT declare detected_host_os(), process_detected_host_os_kb, or any other function — the module never calls them (invariant 17). Declaring them would require linking their implementations.
   CRITICAL GOTCHA (architecture/findings_and_risks.md F4): Do NOT add `#include "usb_device_state.h"`. The real header has it, but the stub must not — it would cascade into undefined types in the host harness. The stub provides ONLY the enum.
4. OUTPUT: qmk_stubs/os_detection.h exists and defines os_variant_t. notifier.c (which includes notifier.h, which includes os_detection.h) compiles in the host harness via `-Iqmk_stubs`. In a real QMK build, the genuine quantum/os_detection.h is pulled in transitively via #include QMK_KEYBOARD_H and provides the real (richer) header.
5. DOCS: Add a header comment explaining: this is a MINIMAL host-test stub providing only the os_variant_t TYPE (the module uses the type only, never detected_host_os() — invariant 17 / §2 F8.2). Note the real header is quantum/os_detection.h and that production builds use the genuine one.
6. MOCKING: This file IS the mock — it mocks QMK's os_detection.h for host compilation. No further mocking needed.

tasks.title: notifier.c multi-OS core
tasks.description: Extend the existing notifier.c (~339 lines) with the current_os global, the 16 per-OS weak accessor functions, the select_command_map_os/select_layer_map_os selector helpers, the notifier_set_os state-clearing function (idempotent + clear-on-change), and the per-track OS-first/default-fallback scan in process_full_message. The file grows to ~410 lines per PRD §8.1/§8.3/§8.6/§8.7.

subtasks.title: Per-OS weak accessors + select_command_map_os/select_layer_map_os selectors
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: The current notifier.c (read at repo root) already has the default weak accessors block (get_command_map/get_command_map_size/get_layer_map/get_layer_map_size with __attribute__((weak)), each returning empty maps or size 0). The new per-OS accessors follow the SAME weak-default pattern but are keyed by ##os-mangled symbol names. See plan/002_c243e735980a/architecture/external_deps.md for the exact os_variant_t values and findings_and_risks.md F1 for the naming contract.
2. INPUT: The os_variant_t type from os_detection.h (via notifier.h, dependency P1.M1.T1.S1). The symbol-name contract from the DEFINE_*_OS macros (P1.M1.T1.S1): the keymap generates `_notifier_get_command_map_OS_MACOS()` etc. via ##os paste; our weak defaults must use these EXACT names.
3. LOGIC: Implement PRD §8.3 exactly:
   (a) Add the current_os global near the other globals (after activated_layer/current_command, per §8.1):
       `os_variant_t current_os = OS_UNSURE;`
       with a comment: push-only — the module never calls detected_host_os(); OS_UNSURE => default maps only (invariant 17, §2 F8.2/F8.6).
   (b) Add 16 per-OS weak accessor functions (4 OSes x 2 map types x {map, size}). For COMMAND map:
       `_notifier_get_command_map_OS_LINUX()` -> NULL, `_notifier_get_command_map_OS_LINUX_size()` -> 0, and same for OS_WINDOWS, OS_MACOS, OS_IOS. Each with __attribute__((weak)).
       For LAYER map: `_notifier_get_layer_map_OS_LINUX()` -> NULL, `_notifier_get_layer_map_OS_LINUX_size()` -> 0, and same for the other 3 OSes.
       These mirror the existing default weak accessors. A keymap's DEFINE_SERIAL_COMMANDS_OS(OS_MACOS, ...) overrides the OS_MACOS pair; unoverridden OSes keep the weak {NULL,0} default.
   (c) Add select_command_map_os(os_variant_t os, command_map_t **map, size_t *size) — a switch on os that calls the matching accessor pair and writes through the out-params. Cases: OS_LINUX, OS_WINDOWS, OS_MACOS, OS_IOS each call their _notifier_get_command_map_OS_* pair; default (OS_UNSURE / unexpected) sets {NULL, 0}. PRD §8.3.
   (d) Add select_layer_map_os(os_variant_t os, layer_map_t **map, size_t *size) — identical structure for the layer track.
   PLACE these after the existing default weak-accessor block and before the activate/deactivate/enable/disable functions.
4. OUTPUT: The selector helpers resolve {NULL, 0} for any OS without an OS-specific map (or for OS_UNSURE). P1.M1.T3.S2 (process_full_message) calls these to get the OS maps before scanning. When os_map size is 0, the OS-first loop runs 0 iterations and falls through to the default map — this IS the backward-compatibility guarantee (no #ifdef needed).
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Comment the per-OS weak accessor block: (a) each returns {NULL,0} unless overridden by a DEFINE_SERIAL_*_OS macro in the keymap, (b) the symbol names MUST match the ##os paste in notifier.h exactly, (c) OS_UNSURE and unexpected values resolve to {NULL,0} so the default map is used. Comment select_*_map_os: 'dispatch by current_os; OS_UNSURE/unexpected => {NULL,0} => default map fallback (§8.3)'.
6. MOCKING: The host stub harness (qmk_stubs/) provides layer_on/off + raw_hid_send; os_detection.h (P1.M1.T2.S1) provides os_variant_t. No additional mocking. The weak accessors compile in both contexts.

subtasks.title: notifier_set_os + process_full_message OS-first/default-fallback dispatch
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: The current process_full_message (read at repo root) does: strlen guard -> memcpy -> disable_command() -> single command_map scan (first-match-wins via found_command_match index + command_found pointer) -> single layer_map scan -> deactivate_layer() -> enable_command/activate_layer -> CONSOLE_ENABLE print -> return. The CONSOLE block indexes `cmd_map[found_command_match].pattern` by the stale single-map variable. See findings_and_risks.md F2 (use command_found->pattern instead after the split) and F3 (backward-compat is structural, no #ifdef).
2. INPUT: select_command_map_os / select_layer_map_os from P1.M1.T3.S1. The current_os global from P1.M1.T3.S1. The existing get_command_map/get_layer_map default accessors. The existing disable_command/deactivate_layer/enable_command/activate_layer/match_pattern functions.
3. LOGIC: Implement PRD §8.6 (dispatch) and §8.7 (notifier_set_os) exactly:
   (a) notifier_set_os(os_variant_t os) — PRD §8.7: if os == current_os, return immediately (idempotent, F9.3). Else: #ifdef CONSOLE_ENABLE uprintf the transition; set current_os = os; call disable_command() (fires prev on_disable if active); call deactivate_layer() (turns off active notifier layer if any). Do NOT re-dispatch the last message (F9.2 — the next focus-change message from the host re-establishes state). This guarantees no layer/command chosen under a previous OS survives into a new OS (F9.1).
   (b) process_full_message dispatch update — PRD §8.6: keep steps 1-3 (strlen guard, memcpy, disable_command) unchanged. Then for the COMMAND track:
       - Resolve (os_cmd_map, os_cmd_size) = select_command_map_os(current_os, ...).
       - Resolve (def_cmd_map, def_cmd_size) = get_command_map()/get_command_map_size().
       - Scan os_cmd_map first (first-match-wins); if a match is found, set command_found and SKIP the default command map (a match in the OS map PREVENTS the default from being scanned — F8.4).
       - If NO match in os_cmd_map (or os_cmd_size == 0 / os_cmd_map == NULL): scan def_cmd_map (first-match-wins).
       Then for the LAYER track — make the SAME OS-first/default-fallback decision INDEPENDENTLY (F8.5): resolve (os_layer_map, os_layer_size) and (def_layer_map, def_layer_size); scan OS layer map first, fall back to default layer map. A layer resolving from the OS map does NOT prevent the command track from using the default command map, and vice versa.
       Then keep steps 6-8 (deactivate_layer -> enable_command if found -> activate_layer if found) unchanged. The ordering invariants (disable-before-scan, deactivate-before-activate, exactly-one-active-layer, first-match-wins) are PRESERVED.
   GOTCHA (findings F2): the CONSOLE_ENABLE debug block currently prints `cmd_map[found_command_match].pattern`. After the split, the matched map could be OS or default. Use `command_found->pattern` (the pointer is already set to whichever entry matched) instead of re-indexing by a stale variable name. Same care if a layer-track pattern print is added.
4. OUTPUT: process_full_message scans OS-first/default-fallback per track independently. notifier_set_os is the sole mutation point for current_os (invariant 17), idempotent on unchanged value, clears state on change without re-dispatch. When no DEFINE_*_OS macros exist, all select_*_map_os return {NULL,0} => OS scans run 0 iterations => default maps scanned => byte-identical to pre-multi-OS behavior (invariant 19).
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Comment notifier_set_os with the F9 contract (idempotent, clear-on-change, no re-dispatch). Comment the OS-first scan block: 'OS-specific map scanned FIRST; a match wins and the default map for this track is NOT scanned. No OS map (or no match) => scan the default map (F8.4). The two tracks decide independently (F8.5).' Reference the symbol-name parity with notifier.h macros.
6. MOCKING: Same host stub harness. To test in isolation, P1.M2.T1.S1 will define DEFINE_*_OS macros in the test TU to override specific per-OS weak defaults. The select_*_map_os and the scan logic are exercised end-to-end through process_full_message.

milestones.title: Host Test Harness, Acceptance & Docs
milestones.description: The dedicated multi-OS host test suite (test_notifier_os.c), the extended stub-test runner that builds+runs both notifier binaries, the full acceptance gate verification (§11.2A/B/C/D all green), and the changeset-level README documentation sync (Mode B). After this milestone, the delta is complete and verified.

tasks.title: test_notifier_os.c — multi-OS selection/fallback/clear host tests
tasks.description: Create a NEW ~150-line host test suite that stub-links against notifier.c (same -DQMK_KEYBOARD_H / -Iqmk_stubs flags as test_notifier_dispatch) and covers all six §11.2D minimum acceptance criteria for the multi-OS feature.

subtasks.title: Write test_notifier_os.c covering F8/F9 (six §11.2D categories)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: The existing test_notifier_dispatch.c (read at repo root) uses DEFINE_SERIAL_COMMANDS/DEFINE_SERIAL_LAYERS at file scope, a `static void ck(pattern, msg, cs, want)` helper printing PASS:/FAIL:, `static int g_pass/g_fail` counters, and a final `Total tests run: N / passed: P / failed: F` summary. It returns g_fail ? 1 : 0. The runner greps `grep -c '^FAIL:'`. Follow this EXACT pattern. The stub layer_on/layer_off write to a file-static g_active_layer in qmk_stubs.c that the test cannot read directly (findings_and_risks.md F6 — use distinguishable callback flags or distinct layer indices for observation).
2. INPUT: The full notifier.c multi-OS implementation from P1.M1.T3.S2 (notifier_set_os, process_full_message with OS-first/default-fallback, select_*_map_os, per-OS weak accessors). The DEFINE_*_OS macros from notifier.h (P1.M1.T1.S1).
3. LOGIC: Create `test_notifier_os.c` that stub-links notifier.c and covers PRD §11.2D minimums:
   (i) OS-specific map selected + default SKIPPED when current_os is set and the OS map matches — verify per track (command AND layer). Use a message that matches BOTH an OS map entry and a default map entry; assert only the OS map's effect fires.
   (ii) Default map used as fallback when (a) the OS map is ABSENT for current_os, (b) the OS map exists but matches NOTHING, (c) current_os == OS_UNSURE.
   (iii) Command and layer tracks fall back INDEPENDENTLY (F8.5): e.g. a message that matches an OS layer but only a default command, or vice versa.
   (iv) notifier_set_os IDEMPOTENT on an unchanged value — no spurious on_disable/deactivate. Call set_os(X) then set_os(X) again; assert the previous command's on_disable was NOT fired the second time and the layer was NOT deactivated.
   (v) notifier_set_os on a CHANGED value clears state: previous on_disable fires, active layer deactivated, and does NOT re-dispatch (no new on_enable from the stale message). Set up an active command+layer, call set_os(different), assert on_disable fired + layer off + no new enable.
   (vi) A default-only configuration (no DEFINE_*_OS macros defined) behaves IDENTICALLY with and without notifier_set_os — backward compatibility. Assert that calling notifier_set_os(OS_MACOS) on a default-only keymap changes nothing (default maps still consulted).
   OBSERVATION STRATEGY (findings F6): use distinguishable callback functions that set separate global flags (e.g. os_cmd_fired vs def_cmd_fired) so the test can verify WHICH map matched. For layers, use distinct layer numbers in OS vs default maps and verify process_full_message return value; if layer identity needs checking, a minimal `stub_get_active_layer()` accessor may be added to qmk_stubs.c (1 line, Mode A — enhances the test harness, not production code). The implementer chooses the cleanest path.
   Use DEFINE_SERIAL_COMMANDS + DEFINE_SERIAL_COMMANDS_OS(OS_MACOS, ...) + DEFINE_SERIAL_LAYERS + DEFINE_SERIAL_LAYERS_OS(OS_MACOS, ...) at file scope to exercise the override. Declare extern for notifier_set_os, process_full_message, hid_notify as test_notifier_dispatch.c does.
   IMPORTANT: this is a SEPARATE binary from test_notifier_dispatch.c (the runner compiles them independently), so the DEFINE_* at file scope do not conflict.
4. OUTPUT: test_notifier_os.c compiles with `gcc -DQMK_KEYBOARD_H='"qmk_keyboard_stub.h"' -Iqmk_stubs -I. notifier.c qmk_stubs/qmk_stubs.c test_notifier_os.c -std=c99` and all 6 categories pass with 0 FAIL: lines. The final summary prints `Total tests run: N / passed: P / failed: F`.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Add a header comment to test_notifier_os.c mapping each test block to its §11.2D criterion (i-vi) and the PRD F8/F9 section it validates. This rides with the test file.
6. MOCKING: The stub harness IS the mock (qmk_stubs/ provides layer_on/off, raw_hid_send, os_detection.h). The DEFINE_*_OS macros at file scope override specific per-OS weak defaults for this test TU only.

tasks.title: Extend run_notifier_stub_tests.sh + full acceptance gate verification
tasks.description: Extend the existing stub-test runner to build+run the new test_notifier_os binary alongside test_notifier_dispatch, and run the complete acceptance gate (§11.2A/B/C/D) to confirm zero regressions across all suites.

subtasks.title: Extend runner to build+run test_notifier_os and verify full §11.2A-D gate
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: The current run_notifier_stub_tests.sh (read at repo root, 45 lines) does 3 steps: [1/3] stub-compile notifier.c -> /tmp/notifier_stub.o with `-DQMK_KEYBOARD_H='"qmk_keyboard_stub.h"' -Iqmk_stubs -I.`; [2/3] link test_notifier_dispatch.c + qmk_stubs.c + the .o -> /tmp/test_notifier_dispatch; [3/3] run it, grep `grep -c '^FAIL:'`, print `notifier dispatch fails=N`, end with `✓ notifier stub-compile gate PASSED` or `✗ ... FAILED`. The .o is shared between binaries (notifier.c compiled once, linked into both test TUs).
2. INPUT: test_notifier_os.c from P1.M2.T1.S1. The existing test_notifier_dispatch.c and qmk_stubs/ (unchanged). The notifier_stub.o from step [1/3].
3. LOGIC:
   (a) Extend run_notifier_stub_tests.sh: keep step [1/3] (compile notifier.c -> .o) as-is. Expand to build BOTH binaries: link test_notifier_dispatch (existing, step [2a]) AND link test_notifier_os (new, step [2b]: `gcc -Wall -std=c99 -I. $OBJ qmk_stubs/qmk_stubs.c test_notifier_os.c -o /tmp/test_notifier_os`). Run BOTH (step [3]): grep FAIL: for each, print `notifier dispatch fails=N` and `notifier os fails=N`. End with `✓ notifier stub-compile gate PASSED` iff both have 0 FAIL: and exit 0. Renumber steps (now [1/4] compile, [2/4] link dispatch, [3/4] link os, [4/4] run both). The .o is compiled ONCE and linked into both (do not compile notifier.c twice). Grows ~45 -> ~55 lines.
   (b) Re-verify test_notifier_dispatch.c still passes UNCHANGED (its default-only behavior is unaffected by multi-OS — findings F5: current_os stays OS_UNSURE, select_*_map_os returns {NULL,0}, OS scan runs 0 iterations, default scanned). If a tweak is needed, keep it scoped to the new OS surface.
   (c) Run the FULL acceptance gate per PRD §11.2:
       - §11.2A: `./run_all_tests.sh` — all 9 pattern_match suites, expect 0 failures (2019+/2019+ assertions; the exact count may shift slightly but must be 0 failures).
       - §11.2B: pathological NFA stress (a+a+a+a+a+a+a+a+a+a+b vs 199 a's) — result=0 in <50ms. (run_all_tests.sh already includes a perf benchmark; confirm sub-second.)
       - §11.2C: six realistic patterns all print 1 (the run_all_tests.sh perf section or a manual /tmp/nfa_real check).
       - §11.2D: `./run_notifier_stub_tests.sh` — both test_notifier_dispatch AND test_notifier_os report 0 FAIL: and end `✓ notifier stub-compile gate PASSED`.
   (d) Verify no new compiler warnings beyond pre-existing ones (the -Wall -Wextra -std=c99 flags in the runner surface them).
4. OUTPUT: run_notifier_stub_tests.sh builds+runs both notifier stub binaries with 0 failures. The full §11.2A-D gate is green. No regressions in the 9 pattern_match suites. The delta is verified complete.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the runner's header comment to mention it now builds BOTH the dispatch and the multi-OS os test binaries. Update the printed summary to show both fail counts.
6. MOCKING: The stub harness (qmk_stubs/) is the mock for both binaries. No additional mocking.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation (README.md multi-OS section)
tasks.description: Mode B (final cross-cutting doc sync): add a multi-OS section to README.md documenting the opt-in nature, the DEFINE_SERIAL_*_OS macros, the notifier_set_os / process_detected_host_os_kb wiring, OS_DETECTION_ENABLE, the per-track OS-first/default-fallback rule, and the backward-compatibility guarantee. Update the test/acceptance blurb to mention the notifier stub suites. This is the final task — it summarizes the whole changeset and depends on every implementing subtask.

subtasks.title: Update README.md with the multi-OS feature section + backward-compat guarantee
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: The current README.md (read at repo root) has sections: Features, How It Works, Setup (3 steps), Usage (Define Command/Layer Mappings, Pattern Matching Syntax table), Companion Projects, Compatibility, Documentation, Performance, Running Tests (with a per-suite count table + test-status blurb citing 2019/2019). The multi-OS feature is entirely absent. The Setup section has 3 steps (submodule, include in keymap.c, rules.mk) with no OS_DETECTION step. See plan/002_c243e735980a/architecture/system_context.md for the README's current structure.
2. INPUT: All prior implementing subtasks (P1.M1.T1.S1 through P1.M2.T2.S1) are complete and the feature is verified green. The README documents what the feature does, how to enable it, and the backward-compat guarantee.
3. LOGIC: Update README.md (Mode B — cross-cutting summary of the whole changeset):
   (a) Add a 'Multi-OS Support (opt-in)' subsection under Features AND a dedicated 'Multi-OS Configuration' section after Usage. Document:
       - The opt-in nature: multi-OS is an overlay; a default-only keymap is byte-identical to the pre-multi-OS firmware.
       - How to enable: add `OS_DETECTION_ENABLE = yes` to rules.mk (single-OS users skip this).

       - The per-track OS-first/default-fallback rule: OS-specific map scanned FIRST; a match wins and the default for that track is NOT consulted; no OS map or no match => default map fallback. The two tracks decide independently.
       - The push-only OS design: the module never calls detected_host_os(); no link dependency on the OS-detection subsystem.
       - The backward-compatibility guarantee: zero DEFINE_*_OS macros => all per-OS accessors return {NULL,0} => default maps used exactly as before.
       - Note host-provided OS is a PLANNED future enhancement (not implemented; §4.7/§14.1).
